metadata.notes: ready→working→ready around a tool-less turn; antigravity's terminal line is an EMPTY PLANNER_RESPONSE (no final text) which the parser maps to turn_done. Driver lacks an elicited wait_turn → gap.

assessment.body:
## Verdict

**agent=yes.** A tool-less round trip is the most basic agy turn. A `USER_INPUT` step opens the turn (parser → `user_message`, working); the turn closes with a `MODEL`/`PLANNER_RESPONSE` carrying NO `tool_calls`, which the parser maps to `turn_done` (ready). Confirmed in every sampled transcript: the LAST line is an empty `PLANNER_RESPONSE` (content_len 0, tool_calls 0) — e.g. `752dfc36` step 7 and `69343e3e` step 39.

**daemon=full.** The visible arc the scenario asserts (`ready → working → ready`) is exactly what `parser.parseUserInput` (working) + `parser.parsePlannerResponse` with empty tool_calls (`turn_done` → ready) produce. The end-signal mechanism is the empty terminal `PLANNER_RESPONSE`, not a `stop_reason` field — but the scenario explicitly accepts any adapter-specific end signal. A caveat: antigravity's terminal line carries NO final answer text (the last assistant prose is the prior planning `PLANNER_RESPONSE`), so unlike claudecode there is no 'terminal text message' — the end signal is purely structural (empty planner). State still reaches ready, which is all the scenario asserts.

**driver=gap:wait_turn.** The scaffolded `driver-interactive.sh` declares `DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"`; `wait_turn` is still a `not_implemented` stub (SEAM 2 unported). recipe-lint flags `wait_turn` as a semantic_gap (not-elicited). The recipe needs `wait_turn` to detect turn completion, so `record` must port the agy turn-done poll (poll transcript.jsonl for a trailing empty `PLANNER_RESPONSE`) before driving.
caveats: Terminal line is an EMPTY PLANNER_RESPONSE — antigravity emits no final 'terminal text' message; the end signal is structural (empty planner with no tool_calls), unlike claudecode's stop_reason=end_turn. State still settles to ready.
Tokens/cost are not in the JSONL (SQLite-only), so observations assert cost/tokens = 0; model is harvestable from <USER_SETTINGS_CHANGE> but left provisional for record to fill from the live surface.
daemon=full is provisional on emission: confirmed against sampled transcripts but not yet a live recording (the empty-terminal turn_done is the load-bearing emission).

sources.note: parsePlannerResponse: empty tool_calls → turn_done (ready); parseUserInput → user_message (working)
last line step_index 7 = MODEL/PLANNER_RESPONSE content_len 0 tool_calls 0 — the terminal turn_done shape
DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"; wait_turn arm is not_implemented (SEAM 2 unported)

preconditions: agy CLI >=1.0.10 on PATH, authenticated via Google OAuth (oauth_creds.json under ~/.gemini)
irrlichd recording (isolated run-cell mode or --attach against a --record daemon)
driver's wait_turn seam ported (poll transcript.jsonl for a trailing empty PLANNER_RESPONSE)
setup: Fresh cwd under <staging>/cwd/; agy mints its own <conv-id> (the daemon session_id) — ignore the preferred UUID
verify: events.jsonl shows the conv-id session entering ready after launch
exactly one state_transition to working and one to ready for the session
